caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) for individuals over the age of 72. The page explains that RMDs cannot be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan and provides a link to an RMD Comparison Chart for further details. It also mentions that Roth IRAs do not require distributions while the original owner is alive. The section titled "RMD Calculations and 50% tax on missed distributions" outlines the responsibilities of IRA trustees and plan administrators, who must report or calculate the RMD amount. IRA owners or trustees must calculate the RMD separately for each IRA they own, though they can withdraw the total amount from one or more IRAs. However, RMDs from workplace retirement plans must be taken separately from each plan. The video likely serves as an informational guide for viewers to understand their obligations regarding RMDs and avoid potential penalties.
